---
title: "Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database - General Classification Guide"
author: "Edited by Jan Jansen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
# site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
output:
  bookdown::pdf_book:
    includes:
      in_header: preamble.tex
  bookdown::html_document2:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true

# bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
# url: your book url like https://bookdown.org/yihui/bookdown
# cover-image: path to the social sharing image like images/cover.jpg
# link-citations: yes
# github-repo: rstudio/bookdown-demo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(scipen=999)

## load libraries
library(imager)
library(kableExtra)

## set directories
sci.dir <- "C:/Users/jjansen/OneDrive - University of Tasmania/science/"
r.dir <- "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/ASAID_data/AnnotationLibrary_AllFinishedSurveys/"

#SO.dir <- paste0(sci.dir,"SouthernOceanBiodiversityMapping/")
#img.cover.dir <- paste0(r.dir,"Cover/")

## load annotation file
dat.pts1 <- read.csv(paste0(sci.dir,"data_biological/SquidleASAIDAnnotations_202602_Points1.csv"))
dat.pts2 <- read.csv(paste0(sci.dir,"data_biological/SquidleASAIDAnnotations_202602_Points2.csv"))
dat.pts3 <- read.csv(paste0(sci.dir,"data_biological/SquidleASAIDAnnotations_202602_Points3.csv"))
dat.pts <- rbind(dat.pts1, dat.pts2, dat.pts3)

## subset to only the relevant columns for the classification guide
dat <- dat.pts[,c("point.media.deployment.campaign.key", "point.media.data.equipment", "point.media.deployment.key", "point.media.key",
                  "label.lineage_names", "point.x", "point.y","point.media.path_best_thm")]
dat$point.media.path.images <- gsub("thumbnails", "images", dat$point.media.path_best_thm)
#path.to.images <- paste0(r.dir,dat$point.media.deployment.campaign.key,"/",dat$point.media.data.equipment,"/",dat$point.media.deployment.key,"/images/",dat$point.media.key)
dat$label.lineage_names[which(dat$label.lineage_names=="1.1 Biota > Worms > Unidentified TemporaryLabelDoNotUse")] <- "1.1 Biota > Worms > Unidentified"
## remove "1.1 " or "2 " from all labels
dat$label.lineage_names <- gsub("1.1 ", "", dat$label.lineage_names)
dat$label.lineage_names <- gsub("2 ", "", dat$label.lineage_names)
dat$label.lineage_names <- as.factor(dat$label.lineage_names)

## which ones are VME taxa
idx.vme.AMC <- c(2:43, 49, 51, 66, 93:111, 116, 120)
vme.AMC <- levels(dat$label.lineage_names)[idx.vme.AMC]
dat$VME <- "No"
dat$VME[which(dat$label.lineage_names %in% vme.AMC)] <- "Yes"

# ## add coralnet labels based on matching AMC ids from ann.transl with ann.cover.dat
# ann.transl <- read.csv(paste0(SO.dir,"ARC_Data/annotation/translate_CN_squidle.csv"))
# ann.cover.dat$CoralNet_ShortCode <- NA
# for(i in 1:nrow(ann.transl)){
#   sel <- which(ann.cover.dat$AMC_ID==ann.transl$AMC_id[i])
#   if(length(sel>=1)){
#     ann.cover.dat$CoralNet_ShortCode[sel] <- ann.transl$CoralNet_ShortCode[i]
#   }
# }

## for each level in dat$label.lineage_names, select 20 random annotations, spread first across as many research campaigns as possible, then across as many deployments as possible
ann.sel.list <- list()
for (i in seq_along(levels(dat$label.lineage_names))) {
  label <- levels(dat$label.lineage_names)[i]
  # message(label)
  label.dat <- dat[dat$label.lineage_names == label, ]
  # Get unique campaigns for this label
  campaigns <- unique(label.dat$point.media.deployment.campaign.key)
  # print(campaigns)
  # Sample one annotation from each campaign
  sampled.idx <- NA
  for(k in 1:length(campaigns)){
    camp <- campaigns[k]
    idx <- which(label.dat$point.media.deployment.campaign.key == camp)
    if(length(idx)>0){
      sampled.idx[k] <- idx[sample(1:length(idx), 1)]
    }
  }
  # If we have fewer than 20 samples, fill up with one annotation from each deployment that hasn't been included yet, skipping over annotations that are already included in sampled.idx
  if (length(sampled.idx) < 20) {
    remaining.idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(label.dat)), sampled.idx)
    deployments <- unique(label.dat$point.media.deployment.key[remaining.idx])
    additional.idx <- unlist(lapply(deployments, function(dep) {
      idx <- which(label.dat$point.media.deployment.key == dep & !(seq_len(nrow(label.dat)) %in% sampled.idx))
      if (length(idx) > 0) idx[sample(1:length(idx), 1)] else NULL
    }))
    sampled.idx <- c(sampled.idx, additional.idx)
  }  
  ## if there are still fewer than 20 samples, fill up with random samples from the remaining annotations
  if (length(sampled.idx) < 20) {
    remaining.idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(label.dat)), sampled.idx)
    additional.idx <- remaining.idx[sample(1:length(remaining.idx), min(20 - length(sampled.idx), length(remaining.idx)))]
    sampled.idx <- c(sampled.idx, additional.idx)
  }
  
  ann.sel.list[[label]] <- label.dat[sampled.idx, ]
}

## the paths to the images online:
image.paths <- unlist(lapply(ann.sel.list, function(df) df$point.media.path.images))
image.paths <- unique(image.paths)
## download all unique images to the local directory "C:/Users/jjansen/OneDrive - University of Tasmania/science/data_biological/Stills/ASAID_annotated_images/"
local.dir <- "C:/Users/jjansen/OneDrive - University of Tasmania/science/data_biological/Stills/ASAID_annotated_images/"

#### code below was used to download all images to the local harddrive
# for(i in seq_along(image.paths)){
#   print(paste0("Downloading image ", i, " of ", length(image.paths)))
#   img.url <- image.paths[i]
#   img.name <- basename(img.url)
#   local.path <- file.path(local.dir, img.name)
#   if(!file.exists(local.path)){
#     try(download.file(img.url, local.path, mode = "wb"), silent = TRUE)
#   }
# }
## quicker to download from the R-drive
remote.dir <- "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/SQUIDLE_dataset_202411/"
remote.dir2 <- "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/SQUIDLE_dataset_fix_AA2011"
# ## and then they are in "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/SQUIDLE_dataset_202411/{campaign}/{equipment}/{deployment}/images/{media.key}.jpg"
for(i in seq_along(image.paths)){
  print(paste0("Copying image ", i, " of ", length(image.paths)))
  img.url <- image.paths[i]
  img.name <- basename(img.url)
  local.path <- file.path(local.dir, img.name)
  if(!file.exists(local.path)){
    ## if the image is from AA2011, use remote.dir2 to download
    if(grepl("AA2011", img.url)){
      remote.path <- file.path(remote.dir2, sub("https://data.imas.utas.edu.au/imagery/IMAS_Antarctic/Antarctica_East_2011_AA2011/CTD/", "", img.url))
    } else {
    remote.path <- file.path(remote.dir, sub("https://data.imas.utas.edu.au/imagery/IMAS_Antarctic/", "", img.url))
    }
    try(file.copy(remote.path, local.path), silent = TRUE)
  }
}


## update ann.sel.list to point to the local files instead of the URLs, and the images on the local drive are all together in the same folder
for(i in seq_along(ann.sel.list)){
  df <- ann.sel.list[[i]]
  df$point.media.path.images <- paste0(local.dir, basename(df$point.media.path.images))
  ann.sel.list[[i]] <- df
}

## Requires:
## - dat: full annotation data.frame (as in your code)
## - ann.sel.list: named list where each name is a label and the value is a data.frame
##                 of up to 20 selected annotations for that label (your selection code)
## - Libraries: imager, knitr, kableExtra

load_and_plot <- function(label.idx = 1,
                          img.count = 16,
                          y_origin = c("bottom","top"),
                          crosshair_len = 12,
                          center_gap = 8,           # <- size of the gap around the center (pixels)
                          crosshair_lty = 1,        # <- dashed
                          crosshair_lwd = 2,
                          crosshair_col = "chartreuse3") {
  y_origin <- match.arg(y_origin)

  # --- Resolve label ----
  labs <- levels(dat$label.lineage_names)
  if (label.idx < 1 || label.idx > length(labs)) {
    warning("label.idx out of bounds.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  label <- labs[label.idx]

  # --- Data for the table & plotting ----
  lab.all <- dat[dat$label.lineage_names == label, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(lab.all) == 0) {
    warning(sprintf("No annotations found for label: %s", label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  sel.df <- ann.sel.list[[label]]
  if (is.null(sel.df) || nrow(sel.df) == 0) {
    warning(sprintf("No selected annotations in ann.sel.list for label: %s", label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # limit to requested img.count
  sel.df <- sel.df[seq_len(min(img.count, nrow(sel.df))), , drop = FALSE]

  # --- Header + summary table ----
  cat(paste0("### ", label, " {-} \n\n"))

  df <- data.frame(
    "Found in campaigns" = paste(unique(lab.all$point.media.deployment.campaign.key), collapse = ", "),
    "Total annotations"  = nrow(lab.all),
    "VME-taxa?"          = unique(lab.all$VME),
    check.names = FALSE
  )

  if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
    cat(knitr::kable(df, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE))
  } else {
    cat(knitr::kable(df, format = "html"))
  }

  # --- Flexible panel layout (default: 4 columns) ----
  n <- nrow(sel.df)
  ncol <- if (n <= 9) 3 else 4
  nrow <- ceiling(n / ncol)
  op <- par(mfrow = c(nrow, ncol), oma = c(1, 0, 1, 0), mar = c(2, 0, 0, 0))

  on.exit(par(op), add = TRUE)

  # --- Helper: plot one crop with crosshair ----
  for (k in seq_len(n)) {
    p <- sel.df[k, , drop = FALSE]

    # Try to load the full-res frame from local.dir
    local.path <- paste0(local.dir, basename(p$point.media.path.images))
    img <- try(imager::load.image(local.path), silent = TRUE)
    #img <- try(imager::load.image(p$point.media.path.images), silent = TRUE)
    if (inherits(img, "try-error")) {
      # Graceful fallback tile
      plot(NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "")
      text(0.5, 0.6, "Image not available", col = "red")
      mtext(p$point.media.deployment.key, side = 1, cex = 0.7)
      next
    }

    W <- imager::width(img)
    H <- imager::height(img)

    # Convert normalized coordinates -> pixel coordinates
    px <- as.numeric(p$point.x)
    py <- as.numeric(p$point.y)

    # y-origin: top (web) vs bottom (R image default)
    py <- if (y_origin == "top") (1 - py) else py

    x_pix <- max(1L, min(W, round(px * (W - 1)) + 1L))
    y_pix <- max(1L, min(H, round(py * (H - 1)) + 1L))

    # 100x100 crop centered on (x_pix, y_pix), with edge-aware shifting
    # Target inclusive window size = 100 px in each dimension
    x1 <- x_pix - 49L
    x2 <- x_pix + 50L
    y1 <- y_pix - 49L
    y2 <- y_pix + 50L

    # Keep width = 100 if the image allows
    if (W >= 100L) {
      if (x1 < 1L) { x2 <- x2 + (1L - x1); x1 <- 1L }
      if (x2 > W) { x1 <- x1 - (x2 - W); x2 <- W }
    } else {
      x1 <- 1L; x2 <- W
    }

    # Keep height = 100 if the image allows
    if (H >= 100L) {
      if (y1 < 1L) { y2 <- y2 + (1L - y1); y1 <- 1L }
      if (y2 > H) { y1 <- y1 - (y2 - H); y2 <- H }
    } else {
      y1 <- 1L; y2 <- H
    }

    # Crop
    crop <- imager::imsub(img, x %in% x1:x2, y %in% y1:y2)

    # Position of the annotation within the crop
    cx <- x_pix - x1 + 1L
    cy <- y_pix - y1 + 1L

    # Resize to exactly 100x100 if needed, and rescale crosshair coordinates
    cw <- imager::width(crop)
    ch <- imager::height(crop)
    if (cw != 100L || ch != 100L) {
      sx <- 100 / cw
      sy <- 100 / ch
      crop <- imager::imresize(crop, size_x = 100, size_y = 100)
      cx <- cx * sx
      cy <- cy * sy
    }

    # Show the crop
    plot(crop, axes = FALSE)

    # ---- Dashed crosshair with a small center gap (simple) ----
    # (Uses your parameters: center_gap, crosshair_len, lty, lwd, col)
    half_gap <- center_gap / 2

    # Horizontal arms
    segments(cx - crosshair_len, cy, cx - half_gap, cy,
             col = crosshair_col, lwd = crosshair_lwd, lty = crosshair_lty)
    segments(cx + half_gap, cy, cx + crosshair_len, cy,
             col = crosshair_col, lwd = crosshair_lwd, lty = crosshair_lty)

    # Vertical arms
    segments(cx, cy - crosshair_len, cx, cy - half_gap,
             col = crosshair_col, lwd = crosshair_lwd, lty = crosshair_lty)
    segments(cx, cy + half_gap, cx, cy + crosshair_len,
             col = crosshair_col, lwd = crosshair_lwd, lty = crosshair_lty)

    # Caption: deployment (you can change this to campaign/key/etc.)
    mtext(p$point.media.deployment.key, side = 1, cex = 0.7)
  }
  cat("\n\\newpage\n\n")
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

# Point-score classification catalog {-}

This Classification Catalog shows example images for all biotic labels used for the point-scoring method in the Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database (AS-AID). This catalog represents a snapshot of the database at the time of publication of the associated data-paper.
The labels used are based on the Australian Morphospecies Catalog, which is an extension of the CATAMI classification scheme. For more information, the CATAMI paper can be found under this [link](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0141039)  

Up to 16 randomly selected images are presented for each classification, alongside a table with the following information:  
  
* **Found in campaigns**: a list of all campaigns in AS-AID in which this organism has been detected  
* **Total annotations**: the total number of annotations for this label  
* **VME-taxa**: whether this label is identified as a CCAMLR Vulnerable Marine Ecosystem taxa  
  
Please direct any enquiries, questions or comments to Jan.Jansen@utas.edu.au

\newpage
